feat(fcp-core): implement FCP Error Taxonomy with AI recovery hints (1n78.29)

Add comprehensive error classification system for FCP protocol:
- ErrorCategory enum (Protocol, Auth, Capability, Zone, Connector, Resource, External, Internal)
- FCP-XXXX error codes with stable numeric identifiers (1000-9999 ranges)
- AI recovery hints for all 26 error variants with context-specific guidance
- category(), error_code(), and numeric_code() methods on FcpError
- Golden vector tests for error code stability
- Category classification tests

Closes: flywheel_connectors-1n78.29

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.27.1","title":"TEST: Registry/Supply Chain Verification + Mirroring Tests","description":"# TEST: Registry/Supply Chain Verification + Mirroring Tests

## Goal
Prevent executing unverified or downgraded connector artifacts - supply chain is the first gate.

## Test Categories

### Manifest Verification Tests
- [ ] Valid manifest signature passes
- [ ] Invalid signature rejected
- [ ] Expired signature rejected (if time-bound)
- [ ] Wrong signing key rejected
- [ ] Manifest CBOR malformed → rejection
- [ ] Required fields missing → rejection
- [ ] Schema version mismatch → rejection

### Binary Verification Tests
- [ ] Binary checksum matches manifest
- [ ] Checksum mismatch → reject installation
- [ ] Truncated binary → reject
- [ ] Extra bytes after binary → reject
- [ ] Zero-length binary → reject

### Platform/Architecture Tests
- [ ] Wrong platform (linux vs darwin) → reject
- [ ] Wrong architecture (amd64 vs arm64) → reject
- [ ] Platform in manifest matches runtime
- [ ] Fallback platform handling (if applicable)

### Policy Enforcement Tests
- [ ] Policy requires attestation → attestation verified
- [ ] Policy requires transparency log → log checked
- [ ] Policy requires specific signers → signer verified
- [ ] Missing required metadata → reject per policy
- [ ] Downgrade attempt detected → reject

### Attestation Tests (when required)
- [ ] Valid attestation chain verified
- [ ] Attestation covers binary content
- [ ] Attestation timestamp within window
- [ ] Attestation revocation checked

### Transparency Log Tests (when required)
- [ ] Entry exists in log
- [ ] Entry matches manifest hash
- [ ] Log consistency proof verified
- [ ] Log inclusion proof verified

### Mirroring Tests
- [ ] Mirror produces expected ObjectIds
- [ ] Mirrored symbols match original
- [ ] Symbol availability correct after mirror
- [ ] Partial mirror handles interruption
- [ ] Mirror respects placement policy

### Version Management Tests
- [ ] Newer version replaces older
- [ ] Downgrade blocked (unless forced)
- [ ] Version comparison is semver-correct
- [ ] Pre-release versions handled correctly

### Audit Event Tests
- [ ] Install produces audit event
- [ ] Update produces audit event
- [ ] Failed install produces audit event with reason
- [ ] Audit event includes manifest hash and signer

## Mock Registry Setup
```rust
let registry = MockRegistry::new()
    .with_connector("fcp.example", version: "1.0.0", signed: true)
    .with_invalid_signature("fcp.malicious")
    .with_checksum_mismatch("fcp.corrupted");
```

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/registry/`
```
valid_manifest.cbor     - Valid signed manifest
invalid_sig_manifest.cbor - Manifest with bad signature
checksum_mismatch/      - Binary that fails checksum
attestation_chain.json  - Valid attestation chain
```

## Logging Requirements
All registry tests MUST emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_invalid_signature_rejected",
  "module": "fcp-registry",
  "operation": "verify|install|mirror",
  "connector_id": "fcp.example",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "manifest_hash": "...",
  "signer_key_id": "...",
  "verification_step": "signature|checksum|attestation|policy",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "FCP-5001"
}
```

### For Install Events
```json
{
  "operation": "install",
  "connector_id": "fcp.example",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "manifest_hash": "...",
  "binary_size": 12345678,
  "platform": "linux",
  "arch": "amd64",
  "install_path": "...",
  "audit_event_id": "..."
}
```

### For Mirror Events
```json
{
  "operation": "mirror",
  "connector_id": "fcp.example",
  "source_registry": "https://registry.fcp.dev",
  "objects_mirrored": 42,
  "symbols_created": 1337,
  "total_bytes": 12345678,
  "duration_ms": 5000
}
```

## Offline Testing
- All tests use mock registry
- No network calls to real registries
- Mock responses are deterministic

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are fully offline with mock registry
- Audit events emitted for all install/update operations
- All rejection paths have specific reason codes
- Supply chain attacks are detected and blocked
- Logging is complete for forensics","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T01:53:15.753498397-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T03:54:51.364023322-05:00","labels":["fcp2","mvp","registry","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.27.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.27","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T01:53:15.765894319-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.27.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:54:51.98427486-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.27.2","title":"[FCP2] Registry Hardening (Optional): Transparency Log + TUF + Sigstore","description":"# [FCP2] Registry Hardening (Optional): Transparency Log + TUF + Sigstore

## Goal
Add optional-but-powerful supply-chain hardening layers that can be enforced by zone policy:
- transparency log inclusion
- TUF root pinning (anti-rollback / freeze / mix-and-match)
- Sigstore/cosign verification (builder provenance)

## Requirements
- These features are **policy-controlled**:
  - high-trust zones can require transparency + attestations
  - lower-trust zones can allow unsigned/less strict sources if explicitly configured

- If enabled by policy, verification must be mechanical:
  - fail closed when required artifacts are missing/invalid
  - produce DecisionReceipts/AuditEvents with evidence object IDs

## Deliverables
- Verification adapters:
  - transparency log inclusion proof verification
  - TUF metadata fetch/verify + root pinning
  - Sigstore bundle verification (publisher/builders)
- Clear reason codes:
  - `supply_chain.missing_transparency`
  - `supply_chain.tuf_root_mismatch`
  - `supply_chain.sigstore_invalid`

## Tests
- Mock transparency log server tests.
- Mock TUF repo tests (rollback/freeze/mix-and-match attempts).
- Sigstore verification tests with known bundles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy can require these checks per zone without changing connector binaries.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.28","title":"[FCP2] Observability: Structured Logs, Metrics, TraceContext Propagation","description":"# [FCP2] Observability: Structured Logs, Metrics, TraceContext Propagation

## Goal
Make the system operable without disabling security. Provide first-class observability that enables debugging denied requests, tracing operations end-to-end, and monitoring system health.

## TraceContext (NORMATIVE)

W3C-compatible trace context for end-to-end request tracing:

```rust
/// W3C TraceContext (NORMATIVE when present)
///
/// Enables stitching mesh routing, connector execution, receipts, and audit together.
pub struct TraceContext {
    /// 16-byte trace ID (W3C traceparent format)
    pub trace_id: [u8; 16],
    /// 8-byte span ID (W3C traceparent format)
    pub span_id: [u8; 8],
    /// Trace flags (sampled, etc.)
    pub trace_flags: u8,
    /// Optional tracestate for vendor-specific context
    pub trace_state: Option<String>,
}
```

TraceContext MUST be propagated through:
- InvokeRequest → OperationReceipt → AuditEvent
- Cross-node symbol routing
- Connector execution spans
- DecisionReceipt evidence

## Structured Logs (JSON - NORMATIVE)

All components MUST log structured JSON with required fields:
- `timestamp` (RFC3339 with microsecond precision)
- `level` (trace, debug, info, warn, error)
- `correlation_id` (16-byte UUID; same as trace_id when available)
- `zone_id` (when applicable)
- `connector_id` + `operation_id` (when applicable)
- `decision` + `reason_code` for policy decisions
- `node_id` (local TailscaleNodeId)

### Redaction Guarantees (NORMATIVE)

MUST never log:
- Secret material (tokens, keys, passwords)
- PII (email addresses, phone numbers, IP addresses outside mesh)
- Request/response bodies containing sensitive data

MUST redact:
- Credential fields in audit logs
- Token payloads (log token JTI only)

## Required Metrics (NORMATIVE)

Implementations MUST expose:

### Request Metrics
- `fcp_request_total{connector, operation, risk_tier, status}` - Request counts
- `fcp_request_duration_seconds{connector, operation, risk_tier}` - Latency histogram
- `fcp_denial_total{reason_code, zone}` - Denial counts by reason

### Revocation/Checkpoint Metrics
- `fcp_revocation_freshness{zone}` - Gauge (0=stale, 1=ok)
- `fcp_checkpoint_seq{zone}` - Current checkpoint sequence
- `fcp_checkpoint_coverage{zone}` - Node coverage fraction

### Store Metrics
- `fcp_symbol_store_objects_total{zone}` - Total objects
- `fcp_symbol_coverage{zone}` - Coverage fraction
- `fcp_repair_actions_total{zone, type}` - Repair operations

### Admission Control Metrics
- `fcp_admission_rejected_total{peer, reason}` - Rejection counts
- `fcp_decode_budget_exhausted_total` - DoS mitigation triggers

### Rate Limiting Metrics
- `fcp_rate_limit_violations_total{connector, operation}` - Throttle events

## Audit Event Integration

All security-relevant decisions MUST be recorded in the audit chain (1n78.11):
- Secret access with SecretAccessToken
- High-risk capability invocations
- Approvals/elevations
- Zone transitions
- Security violations

AuditEvents MUST include TraceContext when present in the originating request.

## Tests

- Unit tests: log lines contain required keys (using a test subscriber)
- Unit tests: redaction works for known secret patterns
- E2E tests: trace_id can be followed across components (invoke → audit → explain)
- Integration tests: metrics are exposed and can be scraped

## Acceptance Criteria

- A denied request can be debugged from logs + DecisionReceipt without guessing
- TraceContext propagates end-to-end (verify via test harness)
- CI can fail if logging contracts regress (schema validation)
- Metrics enable SLO dashboards for availability and latency","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T01:49:54.642773957-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T13:57:22.963228849-05:00","labels":["fcp2","mvp","observability","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.28","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T01:49:54.644227686-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.28","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T01:51:12.483983778-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.3","title":"[FCP2] Canonical Serialization: SchemaId/SchemaHash + Deterministic CBOR + CDDL","description":"# [FCP2] Canonical Serialization: SchemaId/SchemaHash + Deterministic CBOR + CDDL

## Goal
Implement the canonical object encoding foundation used everywhere in FCP2:
- Stable SchemaId and SchemaHash for type binding
- Deterministic canonical CBOR encoding (RFC 8949)
- Schema-hash-prefixed payloads for content addressing
- Signature canonicalization for multi-party verification
- CDDL output for cross-language interoperability

## Background & Rationale

FCP2 requires **content-addressed objects** that can be distributed as symbols, cached, mirrored, audited, and pinned. This demands:

1. **Deterministic bytes**: Same logical object → identical byte representation (for ObjectId derivation and deduplication)
2. **Schema binding**: Type information bound to content (prevents object substitution attacks)
3. **Bounded parsing**: No untrusted-length footguns (DoS resistance)
4. **Cross-language interop**: CDDL descriptions enable implementations in any language

Control-plane messages delivered via FCPC streams **MUST still be representable as canonical mesh objects** (SchemaHash-prefixed deterministic CBOR per this spec).

---

## Normative Rust Structs

### SchemaId (Type Identifier)

```rust
/// Schema identifier (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Uniquely identifies a type within the FCP ecosystem. Used for:
/// - Type discrimination in deserialization
/// - Schema hash computation for content addressing
/// - CDDL generation for interoperability
#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Debug)]
pub struct SchemaId {
    /// Namespace (e.g., "fcp.core", "fcp.mesh", "fcp.connector")
    pub namespace: String,
    /// Type name (e.g., "CapabilityObject", "InvokeRequest", "AuditEvent")
    pub name: String,
    /// Semantic version for evolution
    pub version: Version,
}

impl SchemaId {
    /// Create a new SchemaId
    pub fn new(namespace: impl Into<String>, name: impl Into<String>, version: Version) -> Self {
        Self {
            namespace: namespace.into(),
            name: name.into(),
            version,
        }
    }

    /// Canonical string representation (NORMATIVE)
    /// Format: "{namespace}:{name}@{version}"
    pub fn as_bytes(&self) -> Vec<u8> {
        format!("{}:{}@{}", self.namespace, self.name, self.version).into_bytes()
    }

    /// Canonical type binding hash (NORMATIVE)
    ///
    /// Uses BLAKE3 with fixed-size output to prevent DoS via maliciously large schema strings.
    /// The domain separator "FCP2-SCHEMA-V1" ensures hash isolation from other uses.
    pub fn hash(&self) -> SchemaHash {
        let mut h = blake3::Hasher::new();
        h.update(b"FCP2-SCHEMA-V1");
        h.update(&self.as_bytes());
        SchemaHash(*h.finalize().as_bytes())
    }
}
```

### SchemaHash (Type Binding Hash)

```rust
/// 32-byte schema hash (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Fixed-size hash of SchemaId for:
/// - Prefix on all canonical CBOR payloads
/// - Input to ObjectId derivation
/// - Decode-time type verification
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Debug)]
pub struct SchemaHash([u8; 32]);

impl SchemaHash {
    pub fn as_bytes(&self) -> &[u8; 32] { &self.0 }

    pub fn from_bytes(bytes: [u8; 32]) -> Self { Self(bytes) }
}

impl AsRef<[u8]> for SchemaHash {
    fn as_ref(&self) -> &[u8] { &self.0 }
}
```

### CanonicalSerializer

```rust
/// Canonical CBOR serialization (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Implements RFC 8949 deterministic encoding with schema hash prefix.
/// All mesh objects MUST use this serializer for content addressing.
pub struct CanonicalSerializer;

impl CanonicalSerializer {
    /// Serialize to canonical CBOR with schema hash prefix (NORMATIVE)
    ///
    /// Output format: `schema_hash (32 bytes) || canonical_cbor_bytes`
    ///
    /// Canonical CBOR rules (RFC 8949 §4.2.1):
    /// - Map keys sorted by byte-string comparison of encoded keys
    /// - Integers use shortest encoding
    /// - No indefinite-length arrays/maps/strings
    /// - No duplicate map keys
    pub fn serialize<T: Serialize>(value: &T, schema: &SchemaId) -> Vec<u8> {
        let mut buf = Vec::new();

        // Schema hash prefix for type binding (fixed 32 bytes, DoS-resistant)
        buf.extend_from_slice(schema.hash().as_bytes());

        // Deterministic CBOR (RFC 8949 canonical encoding)
        ciborium::ser::into_writer_canonical(value, &mut buf)
            .expect("Serialization cannot fail for valid types");
        buf
    }

    /// Deserialize with schema verification (NORMATIVE)
    ///
    /// Verifies schema hash prefix matches expected type before decoding.
    /// This prevents type confusion attacks and aids debugging.
    pub fn deserialize<T: DeserializeOwned>(
        data: &[u8],
        expected_schema: &SchemaId,
    ) -> Result<T, SerializationError> {
        // Verify schema hash prefix
        if data.len() < 32 {
            return Err(SerializationError::TooShort {
                got: data.len(),
                min: 32,
            });
        }
        let got = &data[0..32];
        if got != expected_schema.hash().as_bytes() {
            return Err(SerializationError::SchemaMismatch {
                expected: *expected_schema.hash().as_bytes(),
                got: got.try_into().unwrap(),
            });
        }

        // Decode canonical CBOR (bytes 32..)
        ciborium::de::from_reader(&data[32..])
            .map_err(|e| SerializationError::CborDecode(e.to_string()))
    }
}

/// Serialization errors (NORMATIVE)
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum SerializationError {
    TooShort { got: usize, min: usize },
    SchemaMismatch { expected: [u8; 32], got: [u8; 32] },
    CborDecode(String),
}
```

---

## Signature Canonicalization (NORMATIVE)

For signed objects, signers and verifiers MUST agree on identical bytes. FCP2 defines:

### Unsigned View Construction

1. Create an "unsigned view" of the object equal to the object with its `signature` field set to `None` (or removed)
2. For multi-signature objects, also remove `quorum_signatures`
3. Serialize the unsigned view using `CanonicalSerializer::serialize()` (schema-hash-prefixed deterministic CBOR)
4. The signature MUST be Ed25519 over those bytes

### Multi-Signature Ordering (NORMATIVE)

When multiple parties sign the same object:

```rust
/// Sort quorum signatures for deterministic verification (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Before hashing/signing/verifying, sort signatures lexicographically by node_id.
/// This ensures all parties derive identical bytes regardless of signature arrival order.
fn sort_quorum_signatures(sigs: &mut Vec<(TailscaleNodeId, Signature)>) {
    sigs.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.as_bytes().cmp(b.0.as_bytes()));
}
```

### Example: Signing a CapabilityToken

```rust
impl CapabilityToken {
    /// Create signable bytes (NORMATIVE)
    pub fn signable_bytes(&self, schema: &SchemaId) -> Vec<u8> {
        // Create unsigned view
        let unsigned = CapabilityToken {
            signature: None,
            ..self.clone()
        };
        CanonicalSerializer::serialize(&unsigned, schema)
    }

    /// Sign the token
    pub fn sign(&mut self, key: &SigningKey, schema: &SchemaId) {
        let bytes = self.signable_bytes(schema);
        self.signature = Some(key.sign(&bytes));
    }

    /// Verify signature
    pub fn verify(&self, pubkey: &VerifyingKey, schema: &SchemaId) -> bool {
        match &self.signature {
            Some(sig) => {
                let bytes = self.signable_bytes(schema);
                pubkey.verify(&bytes, sig).is_ok()
            }
            None => false,
        }
    }
}
```

## ObjectId Derivation (Integration)

ObjectId derivation uses canonical serialization:

```rust
impl ObjectId {
    /// Create ObjectId from content, zone, and schema (NORMATIVE)
    ///
    /// Uses keyed BLAKE3 for:
    /// - Performance (BLAKE3 is faster and parallel-friendly)
    /// - Privacy (keyed hash prevents dictionary attacks on low-entropy objects)
    pub fn new(content: &[u8], zone: &ZoneId, schema: &SchemaId, key: &ObjectIdKey) -> Self {
        let mut h = blake3::Hasher::new_keyed(&key.0);
        h.update(b"FCP2-OBJECT-V2");
        h.update(zone.as_bytes());
        h.update(schema.hash().as_bytes());  // ← Schema binding
        h.update(content);  // ← Already canonical CBOR (schema-prefixed)
        Self(*h.finalize().as_bytes())
    }
}
```

---

## CDDL Requirements (NORMATIVE)

The project MUST ship a CDDL description of all NORMATIVE CBOR objects:

### File: `FCP_CDDL_V2.cddl`

```cddl
; FCP V2 Canonical Types (NORMATIVE)
; This CDDL describes the canonical CBOR encoding for cross-language interop.

; Schema identifier components
schema-id = {
    namespace: tstr,
    name: tstr,
    version: [uint, uint, uint],  ; major, minor, patch
}

; 32-byte identifiers
object-id = bytes .size 32
zone-id = bytes .size 32
schema-hash = bytes .size 32

; Object header (appears in all mesh objects)
object-header = {
    schema: schema-id,
    zone_id: zone-id,
    created_at: uint,
    provenance: provenance,
    refs: [* object-id],
    cross_zone_refs: [* cross-zone-ref],
}

; Provenance tracking
provenance = {
    origin_zone: zone-id,
    current_zone: zone-id,
    integrity_label: uint,
    confidentiality_label: uint,
    label_adjustments: [* label-adjustment],
    taint: uint,  ; bitflags
    taint_reductions: [* taint-reduction],
}

; Capability token (COSE_Sign1 + CWT)
capability-token = #6.18([  ; COSE_Sign1 tag
    protected: bytes,   ; CBOR-encoded header
    unprotected: {},
    payload: bytes,     ; CWT claims (deterministic CBOR)
    signature: bytes,   ; Ed25519 signature
])

; ... additional type definitions ...
```

## Golden Vectors (NORMATIVE)

The project MUST ship golden byte vectors covering:

### Required Vectors

1. **Schema hash computation**
   - Input: `SchemaId { namespace: "fcp.core", name: "TestObject", version: 2.0.0 }`
   - Expected: `SchemaHash([0x...32 bytes...])`

2. **Canonical serialization + schema hash prefix**
   - Input: Simple struct with known field values
   - Expected: Exact byte sequence including schema hash prefix

3. **ObjectId derivation for key object classes**
   - CapabilityToken
   - AuditEvent
   - ZoneCheckpoint
   - InvokeRequest/Response

4. **COSE_Sign1 capability token encoding**
   - Full token with signature verification

5. **Multi-signature ordering**
   - Input: Signatures from nodes A, B, C in arbitrary order
   - Expected: Sorted order for verification

### Vector Format

```rust
/// Golden vector test case
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct GoldenVector {
    /// Human-readable description
    description: String,
    /// Input data (JSON for readability)
    input: serde_json::Value,
    /// Expected output (hex-encoded bytes)
    expected_bytes: String,
    /// Expected hash/id (if applicable)
    expected_hash: Option<String>,
}
```

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.31","title":"[FCP2] Automation Recipes: Provisioning Interface, OAuth/Webhook Setup, Minimal Human Prompts","description":"# [FCP2] Automation Recipes: Provisioning Interface, OAuth/Webhook Setup, Minimal Human Prompts

## Goal
Honor the project philosophy: **if it can be automated, it should be automated**.

Connectors should not require hand-written setup steps when the system can do them deterministically.

## Deliverables
- A provisioning interface for connectors (SDK-facing):
  - declare required external setup steps
  - run setup flows via host (with explicit approvals)
  - store resulting state as mesh objects (never secrets on disk)

- Recipe objects:
  - OAuth flows (redirect URLs, token exchange)
  - webhook endpoint verification and registration
  - periodic polling schedules / cursors

## Security requirements
- Any privileged setup produces audit events.
- Secrets handled in memory only; zeroize when possible.

## Tests
- Integration tests using mock OAuth/webhook servers.
- E2E scripts verifying a recipe can configure a connector without manual copy/paste.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A connector can ship with a fully automated setup path where the upstream API allows it.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","title":"Revocation System (RevocationObject/Registry/Chain)","description":"## Overview

Implement the revocation system as specified in V2 spec §14.3. This is CRITICAL security infrastructure - the spec explicitly states: "Without revocation, 'compromised device' recovery is mostly imaginary."

Revocations are mesh objects distributed like any other object and MUST be enforced before use.

## Background & Justification

When a device is compromised or a key is leaked, the mesh MUST be able to:
1. Revoke capabilities issued to that device
2. Revoke the device's issuer key (can no longer mint tokens)
3. Remove the device from the mesh entirely
4. Revoke compromised connector binaries
5. Force zone key rotation

Without revocation, security incidents cannot be recovered from.

## Normative Types (V2 Spec §14.3)

### RevocationObject
```rust
pub struct RevocationObject {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    /// ObjectIds being revoked
    pub revoked: Vec<ObjectId>,
    /// Scope of revocation
    pub scope: RevocationScope,
    /// Human-readable reason
    pub reason: String,
    /// When revocation becomes effective
    pub effective_at: u64,
    /// Optional expiry (None = permanent)
    pub expires_at: Option<u64>,
    /// Owner signature (revocations MUST be owner-signed)
    pub signature: Signature,
}
```

### RevocationScope
```rust
pub enum RevocationScope {
    /// Revoke capability objects/tokens
    Capability,
    /// Revoke issuer keys (node can no longer mint tokens)
    IssuerKey,
    /// Revoke node attestation (removes device from mesh)
    NodeAttestation,
    /// Revoke zone key (forces rotation)
    ZoneKey,
    /// Revoke connector binary (supply chain response)
    ConnectorBinary,
}
```

### RevocationEvent (Chain Node)
```rust
/// Hash-linked chain for revocation freshness
pub struct RevocationEvent {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub revocation_object_id: ObjectId,
    pub prev: Option<ObjectId>,
    /// Monotonic chain sequence number (NORMATIVE)
    /// Enables O(1) freshness comparison: seq_a > seq_b ⟹ a is fresher than b
    pub seq: u64,
    pub occurred_at: u64,
    pub signature: Signature,
}
```

### RevocationHead (Checkpoint)
```rust
/// Enables freshness semantics: tokens bound to a rev_head, verifiers MUST
/// have revocation state >= that head before accepting the token
pub struct RevocationHead {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,
    pub head_event: ObjectId,
    /// Sequence number of head_event (NORMATIVE)
    /// Enables O(1) freshness comparison without chain traversal
    pub head_seq: u64,
    pub epoch_id: EpochId,
    pub quorum_signatures: Vec<(TailscaleNodeId, Signature)>,
}
```

### RevocationRegistry
```rust
pub struct RevocationRegistry {
    revocations: HashMap<ObjectId, RevocationObject>,
    bloom_filter: BloomFilter,  // Fast negative lookup
    /// Latest revocation head known for this zone
    pub head: Option<ObjectId>,
}

impl RevocationRegistry {
    /// Check if object is revoked (MUST be called before use)
    pub fn is_revoked(&self, object_id: &ObjectId) -> bool {
        // Fast path: bloom filter definitely-not check
        if !self.bloom_filter.might_contain(object_id.as_bytes()) {
            return false;
        }
        self.revocations.contains_key(object_id)
    }
}
```

## Implementation Requirements

### 1. RevocationObject Creation and Signing
- Owner-only signing (revocations are critical operations)
- Support all RevocationScope variants
- Validate effective_at is in future or now
- Propagate via mesh gossip immediately

### 2. RevocationEvent Chain
- Hash-linked for integrity
- Monotonic seq for O(1) freshness comparison
- Each new revocation creates new chain node
- Support chain traversal for full audit

### 3. RevocationHead Quorum
- Requires quorum signatures from mesh nodes
- Binds capability tokens to revocation state
- Verifiers MUST check token.rev_head <= local.head
- Enables freshness guarantees

### 4. Bloom Filter Optimization
- Fast negative lookup for non-revoked objects
- Size tuned for expected revocation volume
- Rebuild on restart from persistent store

### 5. Revocation Enforcement Points

EVERY capability verification MUST include:
```rust
fn verify_capability_token(&self, token: &CapabilityToken) -> Result<()> {
    // MANDATORY: Check revocation before any other validation
    if self.revocation_registry.is_revoked(&token.object_id) {
        return Err(Error::Revoked);
    }
    
    // Check revocation freshness
    if let Some(token_rev_head) = token.rev_head {
        if !self.revocation_registry.is_at_least(token_rev_head)? {
            return Err(Error::RevocationStateStale);
        }
    }
    
    // Continue with normal verification...
}
```

### 6. Revocation Propagation
- High-priority gossip (revocations supersede other traffic)
- Persist to local store immediately
- Trigger re-evaluation of cached capability decisions

## Use Cases by Scope

### Capability Revocation
- Revoke specific capability tokens (e.g., leaked token)
- Immediate effect on all nodes

### IssuerKey Revocation  
- Node can no longer mint new tokens
- Existing tokens remain valid until they expire
- Used for semi-trusted devices

### NodeAttestation Revocation
- Complete device removal from mesh
- All capabilities issued TO and BY this node revoked
- Used for compromised devices

### ZoneKey Revocation
- Forces zone key rotation
- All zone members must re-enroll
- Used for catastrophic key compromise

### ConnectorBinary Revocation
- Specific binary hash revoked
- Connectors using that binary MUST stop
- Used for supply chain incidents

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RevocationObject with all scope variants
- [ ] Owner-only signature validation
- [ ] RevocationEvent hash-linked chain
- [ ] Monotonic seq numbers for O(1) freshness
- [ ] RevocationHead with quorum signatures
- [ ] RevocationRegistry with bloom filter
- [ ] is_revoked() check at all enforcement points
- [ ] Revocation freshness comparison
- [ ] High-priority gossip propagation
- [ ] Persistent storage with crash recovery
- [ ] Audit events for all revocation operations

## Dependencies

- Core Types (ObjectHeader, ObjectId, etc.)
- Zone Architecture (zone-scoped revocations)
- Capability System (enforcement points)
- MeshNode (gossip propagation)
- Observability (audit events)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T04:44:25.848323653-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T04:47:20.691101782-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-15T04:47:20.691101782-05:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of 1n78.9 (enhanced with detailed V2 spec content)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T04:44:35.262322521-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T04:44:35.503327279-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T04:44:35.752827498-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T04:44:36.0081018-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-i7nk","title":"TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests (FCP-XXXX Codes, Retry Semantics, AI Hints)","description":"# TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate the error taxonomy is stable, code-complete, and machine-actionable (retry semantics + hints) with golden vectors.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for error code system and recovery hints.

## Test Categories

### Error Code Ranges
- [ ] FCP-1xxx (protocol errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-2xxx (auth/capability errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-3xxx (zone errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-4xxx (store/mesh errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-5xxx (connector errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-9xxx (internal errors) mapped correctly

### Error Structure
- [ ] All errors have stable `code` string
- [ ] All errors have human-readable `message`
- [ ] All errors have `retryable` flag
- [ ] Retryable errors have `retry_after_ms`
- [ ] Errors include `details` when applicable

### AI Recovery Hints
- [ ] Top 20 failure modes have ai_recovery_hint
- [ ] Hints are actionable and specific
- [ ] Hints don't leak secrets
- [ ] Hints are stable across versions

### Error Serialization
- [ ] Errors serialize to canonical CBOR
- [ ] Errors deserialize correctly
- [ ] Unknown error codes handled gracefully
- [ ] Forward compatibility for new codes

### Retry Semantics
- [ ] Retryable errors retry correctly
- [ ] Non-retryable errors fail immediately
- [ ] Retry budget is respected

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-imc","title":"fcp.figma: Image export (PNG, SVG, PDF formats)","description":"# fcp.figma: Image export (PNG, SVG, PDF formats) (FCP2)

## Goal
Export design assets from Figma safely:
- render nodes/frames to PNG/SVG/PDF
- download rendered assets with strict bounds

## Scope
- Request render URLs for one or more node IDs.
- Download rendered assets (streamed).
- Enforce size and host constraints:
  - bounded max bytes
  - allowlist content-types
  - deny redirects to new hosts

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.export.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints must explicitly allow:
  - Figma API host(s)
  - the specific export CDN host(s) if distinct
- Avoid logging URLs/tokens embedded in export links.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.export.render`
  - `figma.export.download`
- Streaming download implementation with hash computation for evidence.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - render request formation
  - download bounds + early abort
  - host pinning and redirect refusal
  - content-type allowlist

## Acceptance criteria
- Asset export is safe-by-default and does not allow arbitrary egress.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-imi","title":"fcp.browser - Headless Browser Automation Connector","description":"# fcp.browser - Headless Browser Automation Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 95/100** — Critical Tier 1 meta-connector.

**Why Critical**: This is the **"UNIVERSAL ADAPTER"** that lets agents interact with ANY web service, even those without APIs. Combined with vision models, this enables true general-purpose web automation. When there is no API, the browser connector is the fallback.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Foundation)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 1 Foundation (immediate)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![ConnectorArchetype::Operational]
```
- **Operational**: Navigates, extracts, interacts with web pages

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Browser session state, cookies, auth tokens
- State tracked: `{ session_id: String, cookies: CookieJar, auth_state: Option<AuthState> }`
- Lease required for session fencing

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Native
```
- **Native MANDATORY**: Requires OS-level process spawning for browser
- Sandboxed via seccomp/landlock
- Browser runs in separate process sandbox
- Note: WASI does not support browser automation

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // DYNAMIC: host_allow populated from policy allowlist
    host_allow: vec\![/* policy-defined allowlist */],
    port_allow: vec\![80, 443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```
**Note**: Browser navigation is ONLY permitted to explicitly allowlisted hosts.

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// ALL web content is adversarial
page_content.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::PUBLIC_INPUT
    | TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT
    | TaintFlags::PROMPT_SURFACE
    | TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;

// Form inputs submitted by agent
form_data.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Extracted data inherits source taint
extracted_data.provenance.taint = page_content.provenance.taint;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `browser.navigate` | Risky | Visits external sites |
| `browser.screenshot` | Safe | Read-only capture |
| `browser.extract` | Safe | Read-only extraction |
| `browser.click` | Risky | Triggers actions |
| `browser.fill` | Risky | Submits data |
| `browser.submit` | Dangerous | Form submission |
| `browser.authenticate` | Dangerous | Credential handling |
| `browser.execute_js` | Dangerous | Arbitrary code execution |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Universal Web Access
- Navigate any allowlisted website
- Fill forms and submit data
- Extract structured data (scraping)
- Handle authentication flows
- Screenshot and visual verification
- PDF generation from web pages

### Automation Superpowers
- **Covers EVERY service without a dedicated connector**
- Handles legacy systems with only web UI
- Enables agents to do anything a human can do online
- Future-proof: works with any new website automatically

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Log into this legacy system and export the report"
"Fill out this government form with the provided data"
"Screenshot the competitor pricing page daily"
"Extract product data from this e-commerce site"
"Complete this multi-step web workflow"
"Check if this website is accessible"
"Generate PDF invoice from this web portal"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### ALL Other Connectors
**Universal fallback:**
- When no API exists, use browser
- Legacy system integration
- Complex web workflows

### Google AI/Vision Integration
- Screenshot → vision model analysis
- Visual understanding of pages
- Layout comprehension

### Vector DB Integration
- Scraped content → embeddings
- Web page indexing
- Content similarity search

### Agent Mail Integration
- Web form results → agent threads
- Screenshot reports
- Workflow status notifications

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### Architecture
```
FCP Connector
      ↓
Browser Controller
      ↓
Chromium/Playwright
      ↓
Web (allowlisted hosts only)
```

### Recommended Crates
- `chromiumoxide` — Chrome DevTools Protocol
- `fantoccini` — WebDriver client
- `headless_chrome` — Chrome automation
- `image` — Screenshot handling

### Browser Options
| Engine | Recommendation |
|--------|----------------|
| Chromium | **Preferred** |
| Firefox | Supported |
| WebKit | Limited support |

### Security Hardening
- Browser runs with minimal permissions
- No extension installation
- Disk access disabled
- Network restricted to allowlist

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:web:browser:allowlist` | Allowlisted sites | Navigate + extract |
| `z:web:browser:auth` | Auth-enabled | Can handle login |
| `z:web:browser:restricted` | Minimal | Screenshot only |

### Security Considerations
- EVERY navigated URL is adversarial
- Content extraction requires sanitization
- Form submission is high-risk
- JavaScript execution is DANGEROUS

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — Web interactions

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; DYNAMIC allowlist:
- Policy specifies permitted hosts
- No implicit wildcards
- Subdomain matching explicit

### Credential Injection
- Site credentials via egress proxy
- Session cookies managed securely
- Never persist login state

---

## Capability Model

### Navigation Operations
- `browser.navigate` — Load URL (allowlisted)
- `browser.back` / `browser.forward` — History navigation
- `browser.refresh` — Reload page

### Extraction Operations
- `browser.screenshot` — Capture page
- `browser.extract` — DOM extraction
- `browser.pdf` — Generate PDF

### Interaction Operations
- `browser.click` — Click element
- `browser.fill` — Fill form field
- `browser.submit` — Submit form (Dangerous)
- `browser.authenticate` — Login flow (Dangerous)

### Advanced Operations
- `browser.execute_js` — Run JavaScript (Dangerous)
- `browser.wait` — Wait for condition
- `browser.scroll` — Scroll page

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Navigate to URL
```json
{
  "operation": "browser.navigate",
  "url": "https://allowlisted-site.com/page",
  "wait_for": "networkidle"
}
```

### Take Screenshot
```json
{
  "operation": "browser.screenshot",
  "selector": "#main-content",
  "format": "png",
  "full_page": false
}
```

### Extract Data
```json
{
  "operation": "browser.extract",
  "selectors": {
    "title": "h1.title",
    "price": ".price-value",
    "description": ".product-desc"
  }
}
```

### Fill Form
```json
{
  "operation": "browser.fill",
  "fields": [
    {"selector": "#email", "value": "test@example.com"},
    {"selector": "#message", "value": "Hello"}
  ]
}
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Command serialization
- Response parsing
- Selector validation
- Allowlist enforcement

### Integration Tests
- Mock browser responses
- Multi-step navigation
- Form interaction simulation
- Error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints (allowlist only)
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Content taint propagation
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real web navigation in unit tests
- URL allowlist enforced
- Screenshot capture functional
- DOM extraction works
- Form filling works
- JavaScript execution requires approval
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements
